description: Impact of alternative fuels on cement kiln emissions, trial burn requirements, and regulatory considerations for co-processing.

common_af_types: Solvents and liquid hazardous waste fuels

regulatory_framework.non_hazardous_AF: Generally subject to existing NESHAP Subpart LLL limits. May need permit modification for new AF.
regulatory_framework.hazardous_waste_AF: Subject to 40 CFR 266 Subpart H (BIF rules) or 40 CFR 63 Subpart EEE (RCRA MACT). More stringent limits and monitoring.
regulatory_framework.key_distinction: Whether the AF is classified as a solid waste or fuel under NHSM (Non-Hazardous Secondary Materials) rules (40 CFR 241)

pollutant_concerns.zinc: Tires contain 1-2% zinc by weight. Most zinc incorporates into clinker. Minimal air emissions but monitor clinker zinc content.
pollutant_concerns.SO2: Sulfur content in tires (1-2%) can increase SO2 if raw material absorption capacity is exceeded
pollutant_concerns.PM: Generally no increase if baghouse is properly operated
pollutant_concerns.NOx: May decrease slightly due to lower flame temperature with TDF
pollutant_concerns.metals: Wire in steel-belted tires adds iron; chromium, lead within typical raw material ranges

feed_considerations.maximum_substitution: Typically 20-30% heat substitution in kiln (whole tires via mid-kiln)
feed_considerations.feed_location: Mid-kiln (whole tires), kiln hood (shredded), or calciner (shredded)

pollutant_concerns.THC: Volatile organics in solvents can increase THC if not completely combusted
pollutant_concerns.HCl: Chlorinated solvents (methylene chloride, trichloroethylene) are major HCl sources
pollutant_concerns.metals: Some waste solvents contain heavy metals depending on source
pollutant_concerns.CO: Proper atomization and combustion air critical to avoid CO spikes
pollutant_concerns.D_F: Chlorine + organic carbon + combustion conditions can form dioxins/furans

feed_considerations.Cl_limit: Total chlorine input typically limited to 0.015-0.020% of clinker weight to avoid kiln buildup and HCl exceedances

pollutant_concerns.HCl: PVC-containing plastics are significant chlorine source
pollutant_concerns.CO: Incomplete combustion of large plastic pieces if residence time is insufficient
pollutant_concerns.metals: Depending on plastic type, may contain cadmium, lead (pigments, stabilizers)
pollutant_concerns.THC: Volatile components may release before reaching combustion zone

feed_considerations.size_reduction: Must be shredded to <25-50mm for adequate burnout
feed_considerations.PVC_screening: Screen or limit PVC content to control chlorine input

pollutant_concerns.moisture: High moisture biomass reduces flame temperature and may increase CO
pollutant_concerns.NOx: Biomass generally has lower NOx impact than fossil fuels
pollutant_concerns.PM: Increased ash content may affect PM if not properly controlled
pollutant_concerns.biogenic_CO2: Biomass CO2 considered carbon-neutral for greenhouse gas reporting

feed_considerations.moisture_limit: Typically <20% moisture for direct firing, higher moisture requires pre-drying
feed_considerations.maximum_substitution: Can achieve 30-50% heat substitution with proper preparation

trial_burn_requirements.when_required: When introducing a new alternative fuel that may affect emissions, especially hazardous waste fuels
process: Submit trial burn plan to regulatory agency
Conduct trial burn with comprehensive emissions testing
Submit trial burn report with test results
Apply for permanent permit modification if successful
typical_test_parameters: All NESHAP-regulated pollutants (PM, SO2, NOx, HCl, Hg, THC, D/F)
Metals (Sb, As, Ba, Be, Cd, Cr, Co, Cu, Pb, Mn, Ni, Se, Ag, Tl, V, Zn)
DRE (Destruction and Removal Efficiency) for hazardous waste - must achieve 99.99%
Operating parameters (feed rate, temperature, O2) to establish compliance ranges
trial_burn_requirements.duration: Typically 3-10 days of testing at various feed rates
